findings.description: display_name and action from the DB are interpolated raw into HTML via f-string, enabling stored XSS in the admin dashboard.
The caller-supplied title is injected into PAGE_TEMPLATE without HTML escaping, allowing reflected HTML/script injection.
Docstring promises an inclusive [start, end] range but the query uses created_at < end, silently excluding the entire end day (off-by-one vs stated contract).
save_report writes to a caller-supplied out_path with no path validation, permitting path traversal / arbitrary file overwrite if the path is attacker-influenced.
_connect and all query methods have no error handling for DB-down/locked/corrupt; exceptions propagate raw with no logging or graceful degradation.
fetch_events.fetchall plus full row accumulation loads an unbounded date range entirely into memory and builds one giant HTML string, risking OOM on large ranges.
rows is built via repeated string concatenation (rows += ...) in a loop instead of a list with ''.join(), causing quadratic-ish allocation on large result sets.
save_report does not handle missing directory or permission errors; FileNotFoundError/PermissionError propagate uncaught.
event_count assumes row is non-None; true for COUNT(*) but not defensively guarded — pure style note.
